description: XState as deterministic projection, visualization, simulation, inspection, and local orchestration support while Pibo IR and kernel records remain authoritative.

userStories.title: Add deterministic XState machine projection
userStories.description: As a future editor implementer, I want projection metadata so that UI can display Pibo workflow concepts.
acceptanceCriteria: projectToXState maps workflow definition to XState-compatible machine config

Projection maps nodes to states/invoked actors, edges to transitions, guards to named guards, human waits to waiting states, retry delays to delay states, nested workflows to child actors/machines, and completion/failure/cancel to terminal states
Projection preserves node ids, edge ids, guards, waits, final states, actor refs, tags, descriptions, metadata, and UI hints needed for display
Deterministic snapshot tests cover representative fixtures
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Reconstruct UI snapshot from kernel records
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I want UI state recoverable without XState snapshots so that Pibo persistence remains authoritative.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot kinds kernel, xstate, and ui are distinguished and versioned when persisted
UI snapshot reconstruction works from workflow run, node attempts, events, waits, wakeups, and checkpoints when XState snapshot is deleted
XState context is a projection of global/local/edge state, not a replacement for workflow state records
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Define Pibo-owned actor interface
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I want a Pibo actor contract so that public runtime code does not depend on raw XState internals.
acceptanceCriteria: Define actor interface with start, send, stop, getSnapshot, optional persist, optional restore, and optional inspect
Actor kinds cover agent, code, workflow, adapter, and human
XState actors may implement the interface internally, but public workflow runtime APIs expose the Pibo-owned contract
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Emit workflow inspection events
userStories.description: As a developer, I want inspection events so that I can debug actor transitions and child outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: Inspection event types include actor created, event sent, transition, snapshot, action, child output, wait entered, and wait resumed
Events include runId, actorId, nodeId, edgeId, childRunId, and payload summaries where applicable and authorized
Inspection tests verify required event families emit for fixtures
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Render Workflow/XState visualization data safely
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visual workflow state view so that I understand running, waiting, failed, and complete states.
acceptanceCriteria: Chat Web consumes projection config, current machine snapshot, actor/child hierarchy, guard/action names, tags, descriptions, and metadata
Projection and UI snapshots do not expose raw private payloads unless the caller is authorized for debug
V1 UI does not edit raw XState JSON and full workflow editing is deferred
Tests pass
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Keep XState out of durable execution truth
userStories.description: As a runtime engineer, I want XState to be optional for recovery so that workflow persistence remains Pibo-owned.
acceptanceCriteria: Kernel snapshots and workflow records remain recovery and audit truth
Durable retry/wakeup behavior is driven by workflow store records, not process-local XState timers only
Deep nested parallel statecharts, history states, SCXML-like semantics, and implicit eventless transition cascades beyond simple compiler-generated transitions are deferred in V1
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
